topic.what: Scalable cloud-based biometric authentication system protecting against database breaches by storing only cryptographic hashes of biometric templates
topic.how: Combines ArcFace templatization + OrthoHash locality-sensitive hashing + NIST-approved cryptographic hashing to achieve constant-time O(1) authentication independent of enrolled user count
topic.why_matters: Addresses critical vulnerability in large-scale biometric systems where database breaches expose immutable biometric data; enables practical one-to-many identification without auxiliary identifiers

characteristics.what_it_measures: Query time complexity as function of enrolled users
characteristics.evidence: Hash table lookups are constant-time regardless of database size
characteristics.context: Achieved via cryptographic hash table with hashed bit substrings as keys

characteristics.what_it_measures: Rate at which enrolled users fail to authenticate
characteristics.vs_baseline: ~1.5x increase vs insecure baseline for random expressions
characteristics.evidence: Figure 5 left panel across 1K/10K/100K database sizes
characteristics.context: Performance varies with expression variability; similar expressions yield very low FNR

characteristics.what_it_measures: Rate at which non-enrolled users incorrectly authenticate
characteristics.evidence: Figure 5 left panel shows FPR stays near zero across substring lengths
characteristics.context: FPR remains robust across security parameter choices
Slight increase with larger databases due to cache misses; parallel shard processing recommended

characteristics.evidence: Extended Data Table 1 using Shukla/Daugman method
characteristics.context: High entropy ensures cryptographic hash outputs appear random and protect biometric data

characteristics.what_it_measures: Database storage required per enrolled user
characteristics.context: Requires database sharding per 10K identities for memory constraints

requires: trusted scanner devices with liveness detection
optional TEE for PRF-based entropy remedy

novelty: First scalable privacy-preserving one-to-many biometric auth without auxiliary identifiers
O(1) authentication time independent of enrolled users
First system providing revocability property for biometric enrollment
First demonstration that real-world scalable privacy-preserving biometric auth is feasible
open_problems: Extension to other biometric modalities beyond face
Integration with better industrial-grade templatization models
Reducing storage overhead while maintaining security
Theoretical analysis of entropy requirements for given security levels
